issues: marker taller than player height reference by ~3-4x (appears ~130px screen height vs ~45px per tile)
front-facing perspective sprite inconsistent with top-down world view
scale too large for marker category — should be 50-75% of player, not larger
reasoning: The checkpoint_v05 marker is the tall blue/gold totem figure composited in the center of the scene. In screen space it occupies roughly 40px wide × 130px tall against a tile grid where one tile ≈ 45px — making it approximately 3 tiles tall (~96 game units). The player reference is 32×32, so this marker is ~3× the player height, violating the rule that items/markers must never exceed player size. Additionally the sprite reads as a front-elevation/isometric statue while the world is strictly top-down orthographic, creating a perspective mismatch. The toy-diorama feel is disrupted because the marker dominates the mid-scene vertically and reads as foreground decor rather than a ground-plane object. Combination of oversized scale and perspective inconsistency pushes verdict to fail.
